name: Chandra X-ray Observatory
description: NASA's X-ray eye. Its extreme elliptical orbit swings from 9,942 km to 133,600 km — one third of the way to the Moon. This takes it outside Earth's radiation belts for long, clean observations. Sees black holes, neutron stars, supernovae — the violent high-energy universe invisible to optical telescopes.
scale_fact: Chandra was too large for a direct GEO orbit. Engineers used the highly elliptical orbit as a creative solution — turning a constraint into a scientific advantage.
